skill.name: creativity
skill.description: To transform raw data and abstract problems into novel, feasible, and high-impact creative solutions using analogical "mastication" and multi-stage refinement.
skill.summary: A structured creativity methodology that uses analogical thinking and multi-stage refinement to generate novel, practical solutions.
skill.author: joeloffbeat

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged "Weak cryptographic algorithm" at SKILL.md:3 as high-risk. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The term "mastication" in this context refers to thorough metaphorical processing (chewing ideas), not cryptography. The skill is a pure text-based methodology document with no code, scripts, network operations, or file system access. No security concerns identified.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Term
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged "mastication" as weak cryptographic algorithm. This word means thorough processing/chewing in the creative methodology context, not cryptography. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged term appears in plain text description within YAML frontmatter, clearly referring to metaphorical idea processing, not cryptographic operations. No crypto code or algorithms present.

content.user_title: Generate Novel Solutions Using Analogical Thinking
content.value_statement: Generic brainstorming often produces obvious or impractical ideas. This skill provides a structured methodology for transforming abstract problems into novel, feasible solutions through analogical "mastication" and multi-stage refinement.
actual_capabilities: Deconstruct vague problems into functional essence by removing industry jargon
Apply analogical thinking from distant domains to find unexpected solutions
Generate concrete, feasible ideas using the SUCCESS framework for sticky messaging
Synthesize novel concepts through structured multi-phase refinement
Score and verify ideas for feasibility and utility before final delivery
limitations: Does not execute code or perform technical implementations directly
Outputs ideas and frameworks rather than finished products
Effectiveness depends on the quality of the initial problem description
Cannot access external data sources or perform research independently

use_cases.title: Breaking Through Creative Blocks
use_cases.description: When your team faces a persistent problem with no clear solution, use the ICM methodology to find unexpected connections across unrelated domains.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and team leads seeking fresh perspectives

use_cases.title: Reframing Technical Challenges
use_cases.description: Transform complex technical problems into their functional essence, then apply biological, mechanical, or historical analogies to discover novel approaches.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers and architects exploring architectural decisions

use_cases.title: Creating Memorable Communications
use_cases.description: Apply the SUCCESS framework to ensure ideas are simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, and story-driven for maximum impact.
use_cases.target_user: Writers, marketers, and presenters crafting compelling narratives

prompt_templates.title: Basic Creative Problem Solving
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me solve this problem using the creativity skill methodology. My challenge is: [describe your problem]. Start by identifying the "Why" behind this need.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use case - straightforward creative challenge

prompt_templates.title: Technical Problem Reframe
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the ICM operational protocol to this technical challenge: [describe technical problem]. Use Phase 01 to find the functional essence, then Phase 02 for analogical exploration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use case - reframing technical obstacles

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Creative Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Let us run a multi-agent creative session. Act as The Pattern Hunter to find functional isomorphisms for [problem]. Then adopt The Reflector to identify failure points. Finally, The Storyteller delivers the final narrative.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use case - leveraging sub-role methodology

prompt_templates.title: Idea Verification and Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Take my initial idea: [describe idea]. Subject it to the Reality Audit (Phase 04). Score feasibility and utility. If too abstract, return to mastication phase and add concrete details.
prompt_templates.scenario: Refinement use case - strengthening weak concepts

output_examples.input: Help me come up with a better error handling system for our API
output_examples.output:
Functional Essence: Graceful recovery when things go wrong
Analogical Mastication: How does a beehive respond to a damaged cell? The colony redistributes roles. How does the immune system handle pathogens? Swift isolation and cleanup.
Prototype Narrative: Implement a "healing" pattern where failed services trigger automatic role redistribution and self-repair workflows, inspired by biological immune responses.
Reality Audit Score: Feasibility 8/10, Utility 9/10

output_examples.input: Generate a memorable tagline for our data analytics platform
output_examples.output:
SUCCESS Framework Application:
Simple: Data becomes insight
Unexpected: Your data knows more than you think
Concrete: From numbers to narratives
Credible: Trusted by Fortune 500 analysts
Emotional: The moment you finally understand
Story-driven: Every dataset has a story waiting to be told

Tagline Candidate: "Uncover the story hidden in your data"

output_examples.input: Find a solution for reducing user onboarding drop-off
output_examples.output:
Functional Essence: Building trust quickly in a high-noise environment
Analogical Exploration:
- Medical: How do vaccines build immunity? Gradual exposure, small wins
- Gaming: How do tutorials retain players? Immediate reward loops
- Education: How do good teachers engage students? Meet them where they are

Prototype: "Micro-wins" onboarding - 3-minute achievable milestones with instant gratification, modeled after game tutorials

best_practices: Start every session by clearly defining the "Why" - the underlying purpose behind the request
Allow the mastication phase to explore genuinely distant domains, not just adjacent fields
Apply the Reality Audit rigorously - discard ideas that score below 6 on feasibility
anti_patterns: Skipping Phase 01 (Functional Deconstruction) and jumping directly to solutions
Choosing obvious analogies from the same industry, limiting true innovation
Ignoring the Reality Audit and presenting unfeasible ideas as polished recommendations

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides a text-based methodology you apply through natural conversation with the AI.

faq.question: Do I need to follow all four phases every time?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. For simple problems, Phase 01 alone may suffice. Reserve full ICM for complex challenges requiring novel breakthrough ideas.

faq.question: How is this different from regular brainstorming?
faq.answer: Traditional brainstorming generates ideas in one session. This skill structures the entire process from problem deconstruction through analogical exploration to feasibility verification, reducing the risk of obvious or impractical ideas.

faq.question: Can this skill write code or execute technical tasks?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates ideas, frameworks, and methodologies. It does not write code, create files, or perform technical implementations.

faq.question: What does "mastication" mean in this context?
faq.answer: Mastication means thorough chewing and processing of ideas. It refers to the Phase 02 process of churning functional essence against distant domains to extract unexpected insights.

faq.question: How do I use the Multi-Agent Mode?
faq.answer: Invoke it by asking Claude to adopt specific sub-roles (The Pattern Hunter, The Reflector, or The Storyteller) for different phases of the creative process.
